slides.title: Research Questions
key_points: RQ2: How does the proposed approach address 🔥 **82%** plan approval...?
RQ3: How does the method improve over prior work?
slides.notes: Main research questions this paper addresses

slides.title: Problem Definition
key_points: File localization, plan generation, code synthesis
Success criteria from plan approval to PR merge
slides.notes: Define the paper's target problem precisely

slides.title: Related Work
key_points: Multi-agent coding without human feedback loops
Limited enterprise deployment and user studies
HULA advantage: stage-wise collaboration + production validation
slides.notes: Contrast prior agent designs with HULA

slides.title: Core Idea
slides.notes: One-line contribution distilled into core elements

slides.title: Method Details
key_points: LLM-based file localization and plan generation

slides.title: Experiment Setup
key_points: GPT-4 backbone; Atlassian JIRA + Bitbucket environment
Atlassian internal offline set: 369 issues, 94 repos, 10+ languages
Baselines and metrics: offline HULA, human PRs, SWE-agent; recall, test pass, similarity, PR outcomes
slides.notes: Offline, online, and practitioner evaluation design

slides.title: Results
key_points: 86% file recall vs 31% test pass: localization strength, execution gap
30% internal code similarity: code-quality gap to human PRs

slides.title: Limitations
key_points: Limited correctness evaluation and missing test generation
slides.notes: Critical analysis beyond positive deployment results

slides.title: Takeaways & Discussion
key_points: Deployment feasibility for simple enterprise tasks
Documentation quality as a performance lever
Discussion: context augmentation, richer evaluation, broader transfer
slides.notes: Key insights and open discussion directions
